item_1.title: Default to Server Components:
item_1.description: Most UI is server-rendered by default. Reduces bundle size, improves initial load, and enables direct database access.

item_2.title: Client Components for interactivity:
item_2.description: Only use 'use client' when needed: event handlers, browser APIs, state hooks, or third-party libraries requiring client-side execution.

item_3.title: Boundary strategy:
item_3.description: Push client boundaries as low as possible. Keep layouts and page shells as Server Components; isolate interactivity to leaf components.

item_1.title: Server-first approach:
item_1.description: Fetch data in Server Components using async/await. No loading states needed—data is available on first render.

item_2.title: Streaming with Suspense:
item_2.description: Use React Suspense boundaries to stream partial UI while slower data fetches complete. Critical content renders first.

item_3.title: Caching layers:
item_3.description: Leverage Next.js caching (fetch cache, full route cache, router cache) strategically. Use revalidate and cache: 'no-store' for dynamic content.

item_4.title: Parallel data fetching:
item_4.description: Fetch independent data sources in parallel within the same component to minimize waterfall requests.

ssr.title: SSR (Server-Side Rendering)
ssr.description: Default for dynamic routes. Use for personalized content, real-time data, or SEO-critical pages that change frequently.

ssg.title: SSG (Static Site Generation)
ssg.description: Generate at build time. Ideal for marketing pages, documentation, blog posts—any content that doesn't change per user or request.

isr.title: ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration)
isr.description: Best of both worlds: static performance with periodic updates. Perfect for product catalogs, news sites, or any content that updates on a schedule.

page_components.title: Page Components
page_components.description: Route-level composition, data fetching, layout orchestration

composite_components.title: Composite Components
composite_components.description: Business logic, feature-specific UI, composed from primitives

ui_primitives.title: UI Primitives
ui_primitives.description: Reusable, unstyled or minimally styled building blocks (Button, Card, Input, etc.)

item_1.title: Compound components:
item_1.description: Build flexible APIs using composition patterns. Components work together through context or prop drilling, not rigid parent-child relationships.

item_2.title: Render props & slots:
item_2.description: Use children as functions or slot patterns for maximum flexibility without prop drilling.

item_3.title: Polymorphic components:
item_3.description: Components that can render as different HTML elements or custom components via an as prop.

item_4.title: Co-location:
item_4.description: Keep related components, hooks, and utilities together. Feature folders over type-based organization.

item_1.title: Avoid unless necessary:
item_1.description: Most state should be local. Global state (Context, Zustand, etc.) is justified for: theme preferences, authenticated user data, or truly shared state across distant components.

item_2.title: Server state libraries:
item_2.description: Use React Query or SWR for server state management. They handle caching, refetching, and synchronization automatically.

item_3.title: Form state:
item_3.description: Prefer React Hook Form or similar for complex forms. Local state for simple inputs.

item_1.title: Route-based splitting:
item_1.description: Automatic with App Router. Each route is a separate chunk.

item_2.title: Component-level splitting:
item_2.description: Use next/dynamic with loading and ssr: false for heavy client-only components (charts, editors).

item_3.title: Third-party libraries:
item_3.description: Lazy load heavy dependencies that aren't needed on initial render.

item_1.title: Next.js Image:
item_1.description: Always use next/image. Automatic format optimization, lazy loading, and responsive sizing.

item_2.title: Font loading:
item_2.description: Use next/font for automatic optimization, subsetting, and zero layout shift. Prefer variable fonts when possible.

item_3.title: Resource hints:
item_3.description: Use preload for critical resources, prefetch for likely next-page navigations.

item_1.title: Measure first:
item_1.description: Don't memoize prematurely. Use React DevTools Profiler to identify actual bottlenecks.

item_2.title: When to memoize:
item_2.description: Expensive computations, components that re-render frequently with same props, or preventing child re-renders in tight loops.

item_3.title: Avoid over-memoization:
item_3.description: Most components don't need memo. Server Components can't be memoized anyway.

item_1.title: Vercel Analytics:
item_1.description: Built-in Web Vitals tracking, Core Web Vitals monitoring, and real user metrics.

item_2.title: Structured logging:
item_2.description: Use consistent log formats. Include request IDs, user context, and error boundaries for production debugging.

item_3.title: Error boundaries:
item_3.description: Wrap route segments in error boundaries. Graceful degradation, not white screens.

item_4.title: Performance budgets:
item_4.description: Set and monitor bundle size limits, Core Web Vitals thresholds, and API response time SLAs.

footer.text: This architecture evolves with Next.js and React. Last updated:
